frameworks.name: Signal Detection Theory
frameworks.one_line: Unequal-variance SDT explains metacognitive asymmetries under matched type 1 performance.

frameworks.name: type 1 / type 2 metacognition framework
frameworks.one_line: Extends type 2 analysis to absence reports under matched type 1 sensitivity.

core_question: Why do metacognitive asymmetries persist between visibility manipulations when type 1 performance is matched?
key_assumptions: Type 1 performance can be equated across conditions by stimulus matching
Metacognitive ability is indexed by area under the type 2 ROC curve
Signal and noise distributions may differ in variance
manipulations: Visibility reduction via phase-scrambling vs attentional blink
Detection trial type (hits vs correct rejections)
key_findings: Metacognitive AUC for reporting stimulus absence was higher in the attentional blink condition
Asymmetry emerged despite matched type 1 performance across manipulations
interpretations: Metacognitive differences under equal type 1 performance arise from unequal signal/noise variance
